items.title: What comes next after the landmark BHP strike?

children.content: For the first time in more than two decades, BHP workers in the the Pilbara went on strike. What do we know about how much impact it had and what comes next?

gallery.altText: BHP workers walk off working site.

thumbnail.altText: BHP workers walk off working site.

items.title: Researchers working on artificial placentas for premature babies

image.altText: The foot and hand of a premmie baby with hand holding an adult's hand. Fingers only reach an adult's fingertips.

children.content: The aim of the technology is to give babies born too soon a few more weeks to grow and develop in womb-like conditions, and a better chance of a long and healthy life.

items.title: Drug, dangerous driving charges for Guildford train crash accused

children.content: The 33-year-old driver is accused of driving dangerously and driving with an illicit drug in his system in a crash that injured three people.

image.altText: A smashed train on the tracks

items.title: 'Nature needs them more': Scientists warn against taking shells home

children.content: WA's southern coastline is covered in seashells, much to the delight of collectors. However, the state has a bag limit on how many you can take home.

image.altText: a white and orange cone-shaped seashell on beach sand

items.title: Did you know Australia's first live telecast was in Carnarvon?

children.content: Carnarvon played host to Australia's first-ever live telecast with London 60 years ago. Now, one couple has visited the Pilbara town to recreate the family's involvement in the event.

gallery.altText: Three families standing with former ABC presenter Kim Corcoran during the first ever live telecast by Australia from Carnarvon.

thumbnail.altText: Three families standing with former ABC presenter Kim Corcoran during the first ever live telecast by Australia from Carnarvon.

items.title: WA communities still recovering from 'worst cyclone season ever'

image.altText: A large tree has fallen. It is next to a building.

children.content: Two cyclones in less than a month caused significant damage to the Dampier Peninsula in the Kimberley region during the 2025-26 wet season. Months later, residents say costly repairs are continuing.

items.title: Parents fear for school's future with high turnover of staff

children.content: A remote school 500km south-east of Perth is struggling to retain staff as housing pressures force incoming teachers to live far from work.

image.altText: Munglinup to lose a school bus service

items.title: Hanson spotted at Dolce & Gabbana fashion show with Gina Rinehart — as it happened

image.altText: Hanson stands in a black dress with flowers on it

items.title: Relief for marine experts as whale helped out of shallow estuary

children.content: A juvenile southern right whale has been saved by a pod of dolphins after it was stranded in an estuary in WA's south for two weeks.

items.title: Cross-border child protection hub aims to keep kids connected to families

children.content: A new hub to support families caught between three child protection agencies has been established in Alice Springs in a 12-month trial.

items.title: WA home ownership rate grows as Sydney hits 70-year low

children.content: Sydney is no longer affordable for most, as new data reveals the number of people who own the home they live in is the lowest it has been in decades.

image.altText: Generic apartments.

items.title: Stink bomb attack planners taken to court by energy giant Woodside

children.content: Energy giant Woodside has taken climate activists to court in a bid to unmask the conspirators behind a stink-bomb protest that forced the evacuation of its Perth headquarters.

image.altText: Kristen and Joana stand side by side outside court while looking straight at the camera

items.title: Carnival atmosphere as BHP staff down tools for landmark strike

children.content: Workers at the mining giant's operations in Port Hedland have walked off the job in one of the most significant industrial disputes to hit the resources sector in a quarter of a century.

articles.title: BHP hits production record prior to landmark industrial action

children.content: As hundreds of BHP workers prepare to down tools in a landmark strike, the miner has revealed its highest-ever levels of iron ore production.

image.altText: An image of iron ore operations including loaders and conveyer belts

items.title: Parents go against court orders after children 'traumatised'

children.content: The Family Court granted John full custody of his kids, but he returned them to their mother, saying the judge got it wrong.

items.title: Treasurer says AI key to raising productivity, lowering interest rates

children.content: The Treasurer has given an insight into his April meeting with Anthropic, arguing that Australia embracing AI is crucial for turning around the nation's lacklustre productivity and economic performance over recent decades.

image.altText: A tight shot of Jim Chalmers head, he wears a suit and red tie stands in front of a blue curtain

items.title: Perth man who was jailed in China says Australia should do more for citizens detained overseas

children.content: Australians who have endured prolonged detention overseas call on the government to adopt a more strategic approach to cases involving citizens, especially those held by countries that practice "hostage diplomacy".

items.title: Rohan Dennis pleads guilty to driving while disqualified

items.title: Seven-kilometre artwork creates colour journey through Perth suburbs
